mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 6% signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 6-month moving average signals a pickup in economic activity, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) above their respective 3-month moving averages signals a pickup in demand, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS rise above 3-month moving averages
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in joint demand pickup periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 2% signals an easing monetary cycle, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 6% and an increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above its 3-month moving average signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6% and PAYEMS rises above 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in joint labor market tightening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in GDP (GDPC1) above its 6-month moving average signals a pickup in economic activity, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal.
